artPrompt: An empty council dais looms tall in the frame, a long bench of vacant chairs above a podium, while a slow arcane cyan glow seeps up from a stack of blank unmarked forms held in suspended review. Nothing is decided; everything waits. The composition climbs toward shadowed fluorescent ceiling, washes bleeding ivory into cool teal. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Election Certification Board
text:
Whenever an opponent votes, holds an election, or casts a spell that mentions voting, that vote does not count until you certify it. You may decline to certify it. (The Board is taking the matter under advisement.)
Whenever you certify or decline a vote, draw a card.
flavorText: The Mayor was elected in a landslide. The Board has been reviewing that landslide since.
